DISCOVERY 302, GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE REQUEST FOR AN ORDER ESTABLISHING ADMISSIONS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF RECEIVED RESPONSES THAT ARE IN SUBSTANTIAL COMPLIANCE WITH C.C.P. SECTION 2033.220 PRIOR TO THE HEARING AND DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO SERVE TIMELY RESPONSES WAS THE RESULT OF EXCUSABLE MISTAKE. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $900 PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 2033.280(C). DEFENDANT AND HIS COUNSEL ARE JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY LIABLE. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jack Schwartz, Esq., Telephone (650) 863-5823, appearing for Plaintiff Sylvia Sabel;
Kevin Sullivan, Esq., Telephone (415) 860-2170, appearing for Defendant, Andrew Dimitriuo, et al.,

ARGUED. Hearing is held. The Court having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF SYLVIA SABEL'S Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions.

GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The request for an order establishing admissions is denied. Plaintiff received responses that are in substantial compliance with C.C.P. section 2033.220 prior to the hearing and Defendant's failure to serve timely responses was the result of excusable mistake. The request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $900 pursuant to C.C.P. section 2033.280(c). Defendant and his counsel are jointly and severally liable. Plaintiff's counsel to prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/CVA)
